name: Dockable Intent
summary: The recursive partition-tree (docking / tiling) layout model — a node is a `row`, `column`, or `stack`, nesting arbitrarily, with space fully partitioned (no gaps, no overlaps). Splits resize their siblings (the `resizable` linear-split divider applied recursively); `stack` leaves are tab-stacks (the tabs block); panels drag-to-dock between stacks (reorder's grab/move/drop + live-region + `moveBefore` substrate, #1384). Ratified #1437 Fork 1a as a STANDALONE intent COMPOSING `resizable` + tabs + reorder — distinct from `arrangeable` (flat free-placement grid with collision/compaction): a recursive tree state shape that structurally cannot be a flat array. Adds no new gesture. Composed a11y contract: APG Window Splitter on the splits + APG Tabs on the stacks, both fixed invariants. The serialized layout tree is a separate first-class Protocol (#1437 Fork 2a, materializing with the realizing build).

orientation.description: The default split axis of the root container — `row` lays children out horizontally (left-to-right panes), `column` vertically (stacked panes). Per-node orientation is part of the tree state; this dimension only seeds the root. Most-permissive default: absent → `row` (the common side-by-side workspace).

sizing.description: How a node's size within its parent is expressed and persisted — `ratio` keeps proportional weights stable across container resizes (the responsive default, mirroring `resizable.unit=percent`); `pixel` fixes a node's size and lets siblings absorb the slack. Per-node min/max constraints are numeric properties of the binding, not enum values — they bound each split's travel and feed the Window Splitter `aria-valuemin`/`aria-valuemax`. Most-permissive default: `ratio`.

popout.description: Whether a panel/stack can pop out into a separate OS window — `none` keeps the whole layout in-page (the partition tree is complete without it); `window` relocates a live subtree to another `Window` via `window.open()` + `adoptedStyleSheets`. Minimal-core default `none` (NOT most-permissive — the most-permissive value `window` widens the core with a cross-window capability most consumers won't use; #1437 Fork 3a). Cross-window relocation breaks `moveBefore` (cannot move across documents) and the single-document live-region/roving-tabindex wiring — handled in the realizing build, not the contract.

description:
What This Is

The recursive partition-tree docking / tiling model (golden-layout / dockview / FlexLayout / rc-dock family), ratified by #1437 Fork 1a as a standalone intent. A node is a row, column, or stack; nesting is arbitrary; space is fully partitioned — no gaps, no overlaps. This is not a fourth primitive: it is a recursive composition of two node kinds WE already standardizes.

The model

  • Splits (row/column): the resizable linear-split divider applied recursively — dragging a separator reflows its siblings, no collision.
  • Stacks: leaf containers whose children are tabbed (the tabs block).
  • Drag-to-dock: panels move between stacks via reorder's grab / move / drop + live-region + moveBefore substrate (#1384). Adds no new gesture.

Distinct from arrangeable

Where arrangeable is a flat free-placement grid — Array<{x,y,w,h}> with collision / compaction — dockable is a recursive tree with no free placement. The two state shapes are incompatible, which is why this is a separate intent, not a dimension of arrangeable (bias-toward-separation; #1437 Fork 1).

Dimensions

  • orientation: row (default, side-by-side) vs column — seeds the root split axis.
  • sizing: ratio (stable proportional weights — default) vs pixel (fixed size, siblings absorb slack).
  • popout: none (in-page — minimal-core default) vs window (relocate a subtree to an OS window). Deferred to the realizing build (#1437 Fork 3a).

Fixed a11y invariants — composed

Keyboard operability is part of the contract, not an enhancement, and no new pattern is invented — both are borrowed verbatim: WAI-ARIA APG Window Splitter on every split divider (role="separator" + aria-valuenow/min/max + Arrow / Home / End), and WAI-ARIA APG Tabs on every stack (roving tabindex, aria-controls, arrow navigation). Both are fixed invariants, not dimensions.

Substrate & serialization

Built on the same Pointer Events substrate as its composed parts (the drag recognized via the gesture intent's pan; the re-tile is the effect). The saved layout tree (node = {type: row|column|stack, children|tabs, size}) is a separate first-class WE Protocol (#1437 Fork 2a) so the docking impl is swappable and the layout portable — it materializes with the deferred realizing build, not here. Grounded in the docking / tiling partition-tree survey.
